About Phaedon


At Phaedon, we are revolutionizing how companies engage with their audiences and are passionate about sparking participation to drive growth for both our clients and our employees. Phaedon is a data-driven, tech-enabled marketing organization with a breadth of capabilities that go beyond our competitors and redefine traditional industry archetypes. We have a history of delivering game-changing results for notable brands across advanced analytics, loyalty technology and services, marketing communications, and experience transformation to bring intelligent imagination and solve our clients’ biggest business and brand challenges. And every role at Phaedon has an integral part to play in fulfilling this vision.


About the Role:


This is an excellent opportunity for an innovator and problem solver who thrives at the intersection of AI, cloud engineering, and product development. We are seeking an AI/Data Science Product Engineer to join our analytics and product development team. You will be a key member of our team, responsible for building, deploying, and scaling AI-powered data products and analytics solutions that solve real client challenges.


At Phaedon, we recognize that exceptional talent comes with diverse experiences. The final title and compensation will be thoughtfully determined using factors including but not limited to relevant work experience, skills, certifications and competencies that align to the specified role.


Essential Duties/Responsibilities:


Product Engineering & AI Implementation:

Design, build, and deploy AI-powered data products and analytics solutions using cutting-edge technologies
Implement and productionalize AI/ML models using AWS Bedrock and other AI services
Transform proof-of-concept work into robust, production-ready systems
Develop RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems for AI applications
Build cloud-native applications with focus on scalability, reliability, and performance

Client Service & Analytics Delivery:

Work directly with clients to understand business requirements and translate them into product requirements and technical solutions
Present product requirements, user flows, and technical findings and recommendations to client stakeholders with clarity and professionalism
Conduct exploratory data analysis and deliver actionable insights through clear visualizations and reports
Collaborate with client teams to ensure successful implementation and adoption of analytics solutions

Cloud Infrastructure & Data Engineering:

Architect and maintain data lake solutions using AWS native tools including Lake Formation, Glue, and Athena
Develop automation and monitoring solutions using Lambda and other AWS services
Create and maintain Infrastructure-as-Code (IAC) using Terraform, Pulumi and CloudFormation
Build data pipelines that synthesize complex datasets from multiple sources into actionable insights

Quality & Production Focus:

Implement error-proofing strategies and robust testing frameworks including integration testing
Develop CI/CD pipelines for automated deployment and model versioning
Create monitoring and logging solutions for production AI systems
Ensure security best practices for AI applications and data handling

Location:


This role is based out of our office in the Designer’s Guild building in the heart of Minneapolis' North Loop neighborhood. We embrace a hybrid model with three in-office days per week to ensure a mix of collaboration and flexibility to support our employees' success.


Basic Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in data science, computer science, computer engineering, or related field
5+ years of hands-on experience in relevant skillsets including Python development and cloud engineering
Strong experience with AWS services, particularly Bedrock, SageMaker, Lambda, Glue, and Athena
Proficiency in AI implementation, model deployment, and productionalization
Proven experience of authoring product requirement documents, user workflows, and technical flows
Knowledge of Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform, CloudFormation)
Experience with source control, automated deployment pipelines (Git, Docker)
Proven experience building and deploying ML models with various methodologies
Client-facing experience in analytics or consulting environment
Strong presentation and communication skills for technical and non-technical audiences

Technical Skills:

Programming: Advanced Python (including AI/ML libraries like transformers, langchain), SQL, Boto3
AI/ML Tools: AWS Bedrock, SageMaker, prompt engineering, model fine-tuning
Cloud Services: AWS ecosystem with focus on data and AI services
Visualization: Experience with Tableau, PowerBI, or QuickSight
Development Practices: Object-oriented programming, testing frameworks, CI/CD

Preferred Skills:

Experience with vector databases and RAG implementations
Knowledge of LLM fine-tuning and deployment strategies
Familiarity with MLOps practices and model versioning
Experience with API development and microservices architecture
Background in professional services or consulting environments
Experience with AWS data lake tools and architecture
Previous experience in client service roles within analytics or technology consulting

Please note that we are not offering sponsorship of employment visas for this role. To be considered for this opportunity, candidates must currently be legally authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H1B status, 0-1, TN, CPT, OPT, etc.).


What we Offer:


We value our employees and demonstrate this through our comprehensive benefits offering including medical/dental/vision coverage, comprehensive paid time off, paid holidays, paid parental leave, retirement savings plans, and more.


Pay Range:


The pay range for this position is estimated to be: $120,000-185,000 per year.

About Alpha Recon

Alpha Recon is a rapidly growing SaaS company that is disrupting the risk technology and security management space. We will be helping to save lives.

Alpha Recon strives to be the most trusted threat and risk intelligence source in the security world. We provide real-time, valuable, and actionable insights gained by integrating elusive data inputs, proprietary analysis, expert human intellect, artificial intelligence & machine learning, and predictive analytics.

The candidate must be entrepreneurial, motivated, well organized and willing to work for stock options initially! Company milestones will trigger salary, but salary will be zero to start. Please don't apply if you require immediate salary. There are strong business opportunities already in place and we will offer salary based on agreed upon company milestones. We prefer entrepreneurial minded candidates that wish to be with the company long term.

About the Opportunity

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced AI Architect specializing in Generative AI to join our team. In this pivotal role, you will be responsible for designing and leading the implementation of scalable, secure, and innovative AI solutions that leverage the power of Generative AI to solve complex business problems. You will bridge the gap between business needs and technical capabilities, providing strategic guidance and hands-on expertise to drive our Generative AI initiatives from concept to production. This is a senior-level position for a visionary technologist who can not only design the future of our AI systems but also guide a team to build them.

Key Responsibilities

Architectural Design: Design and develop end-to-end Generative AI solutions, including data pipelines, model deployment strategies, and integration with existing business and IT infrastructure. This includes architecting systems for Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), and developing multi-agent systems.
Strategic Planning: Translate business requirements into technical specifications and a comprehensive AI strategy. Work with key stakeholders to identify high-impact use cases for Generative AI and create a roadmap for implementation.
Technology Selection & Evaluation: Evaluate and select the most suitable Generative AI models, frameworks, tools, and cloud platforms to achieve optimal performance, cost-efficiency, and scalability.
Technical Leadership & Mentorship: Provide technical leadership and guidance to data scientists, machine learning engineers, and software developers. Lead code reviews, promote best practices, and mentor junior team members.
Deployment & MLOps: Oversee the entire lifecycle of Generative AI projects, from model development and experimentation to deployment and ongoing maintenance. Define and deliver CI/CD pipelines for GenAI solutions, ensuring best practices for monitoring, governance, and security (LLM-Ops).
Collaboration: Act as a key liaison between technical teams and business stakeholders, communicating complex technical concepts in an accessible way. Work closely with data, product, and security teams to ensure solutions are robust, compliant, and deliver tangible business value.
Research & Innovation: Stay current with the latest trends, research, and advancements in Generative AI technologies and algorithms. Proactively identify new opportunities to leverage AI to drive innovation and maintain a competitive edge.
Ethical AI: Ensure that all AI solutions are designed and implemented in adherence to ethical guidelines and best practices, focusing on fairness, privacy, and data security.

Required Qualifications

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or a related field.
8+ years of experience in software development, with at least 3-5 years in an AI/ML Architect or a similar leadership role.
Proven hands-on experience designing, implementing, and deploying production-level Generative AI solutions.
Proficiency in programming languages, especially Python, and a strong command of relevant libraries and frameworks.
Demonstrated experience with at least one major cloud platform and its AI/ML services.
Solid understanding of MLOps principles and tools for managing the lifecycle of AI models in production.
Exceptional communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills with the ability to lead and influence cross-functional teams.

Deep expertise in foundational Generative AI concepts, including:

Large Language Models (LLMs) and their architectures
Prompt engineering and fine-tuning models
Techniques like Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
Vector databases and embedding models.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in designing and deploying multi-agent systems.
Knowledge of containerization and orchestration tools (Docker, Kubernetes).
Experience with big data technologies
A strong portfolio of deployed Generative AI projects or open-source contributions.

Job Types: Full-time, Contract

Work Location: Remote

Position: 90-Day AI Engineering Internship
Location: On-Site – Hudson, FL (Must be able to commute)

About the Role

Join a growing startup focused on building a cutting-edge artificial intelligence platform that learns from user interactions, controls digital environments, and scales across decentralized systems. Over 90 days, you’ll contribute to real engineering challenges spanning machine learning, data engineering, model deployment, and prototype development. You will work side-by-side with experienced AI engineers in our Hudson, FL office, gaining hands-on experience and mentorship in a fast-paced, innovation-driven environment.

Who We’re Looking For

We welcome driven learners who thrive on tackling complex AI challenges and are eager to build foundational skills. Ideal candidates include:

Machine Learning Enthusiasts (NLP, Computer Vision, or both)
Data Engineers & Data Scientists
MLOps / Infrastructure Curious Minds
Backend Developers (Python-centric)
Frontend Developers (JavaScript frameworks)
AI Research Interns (Literature reviews + prototyping)
QA & Testing Advocates (Automation + model validation)
UI/UX Designers (Crafting AI-focused interfaces)

If you enjoy working with Python-based ML libraries (TensorFlow, PyTorch), building data pipelines, or designing intuitive AI dashboards—this internship is for you. No single candidate is expected to fill every role; focus on your strongest skills and a willingness to learn adjacent areas.

What You’ll Do

Prototype & Train Core AI Models
Assist in training language, vision, or multimodal models.
Fine-tune pre-trained architectures on curated datasets.
Build rapid prototypes (e.g., voice control modules, interactive demos).
Build & Maintain Data Pipelines
Ingest, clean, and preprocess diverse data sources.
Automate ETL workflows for continuous model retraining.
Implement data validation checks to ensure quality.
Support MLOps & Deployment
Containerize models using Docker (optionally Kubernetes).
Configure CI/CD pipelines for automated testing and deployment.
Monitor model performance metrics and logs.
Develop Backend & Frontend Components
Create RESTful API endpoints to serve AI model predictions.
Build on-site front-end demos (React, Vue, or similar) to showcase AI features.
Integrate user feedback loops for continuous learning.
Conduct Research & Documentation
Perform literature reviews on emerging AI techniques.
Summarize findings in clear, concise documentation.
Propose prototype improvements based on research insights.
Implement Testing & QA
Write unit and integration tests for model inference code.
Develop test suites to validate accuracy, latency, and robustness.
Collaborate with peers to address performance bottlenecks.
Participate in Weekly Demo Sessions
Present progress, challenges, and lessons learned every week.
Gather feedback from on-site senior engineers to refine your approach.
Adjust project scope based on sprint goals and timelines.

Qualifications & Skills

We’re looking for interns who meet most of the following:

Academic Background:
Enrolled in (or recently graduated from) a degree program in Computer Science, Data Science, AI/ML, Software Engineering, or a related field.
Technical Skills: (at least two of the following)
ML Frameworks: TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn.
Programming Languages: Python (required); JavaScript/TypeScript is a plus.
Data Engineering: Pandas, NumPy, SQL, ETL pipelines.
MLOps/DevOps: Docker, Kubernetes, AWS/GCP/Azure, Terraform (or similar).
Frontend Development: React, Vue.js, or comparable frameworks.
Computer Vision / NLP: OpenCV, Hugging Face Transformers, spaCy, or other libraries.
Version Control: Proficient with Git and GitHub workflows.
Soft Skills:
Strong problem-solving instincts and attention to detail.
Clear, concise communication—able to explain complex concepts simply.
Self-motivated, able to work collaboratively in an on-site environment.
Eagerness to learn, adapt, and take ownership of tasks.
Bonus (Not Required):
Experience with blockchain or decentralized systems.
Previous open-source contributions in AI, data engineering, or DevOps.
Exposure to UI/UX design principles for AI applications.

Internship Details

Duration: 90 days, full-time (20–30 hours/week).
Location: On-site at our Hudson, FL office (must be able to commute).
Compensation: Salary contract after 90-day term.
Perks include:
Mentorship from experienced AI engineers and researchers.
Access to on-site compute resources and cloud credits for experiments.
Hands-on portfolio pieces demonstrating production-grade AI work.
Letter of recommendation upon successful completion.
Post-Internship Opportunity:
After 90 days, high-performing interns will be offered the choice to join the team as a paid employee (salary) or receive equity in the startup. This is your chance to grow with us and become a core member of a fast-growing AI venture.
Commitment & Schedule:
Weekly sprint planning, demos, and retrospectives conducted in-person.
Midpoint review at 45 days and a final project showcase at 90 days.
Expected start: July 2025 (exact date is flexible).Why Join This Internship?
Real Impact: Your work will shape core AI capabilities—no peripheral tasks.
Accelerate Your Skills: Gain mastery of ML Ops, data pipelines, model fine-tuning, and end-to-end AI systems.
Collaborative Environment: Work side-by-side with peers and seasoned mentors in our Florida office.
Career Launchpad: Top performers often transition into paid positions, equity roles, or leadership tracks within the startup.

How to Apply

Submit the following via Indeed (attach as separate files or combined PDF):

Resume / CV detailing relevant coursework, projects, and technical skills.
One-Page Cover Letter that includes:
Why you’re passionate about AI engineering.
Your preferred technical focus area(s) (e.g., ML research, MLOps, data engineering, front-end integration).
A brief summary of one past AI/ML project where you solved a concrete problem.
Portfolio / GitHub Links (if available) showcasing code samples, models, or demos.

Subject Line: 90-Day AI Engineering Internship – [Your Name] – [Primary Role Interest]

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted within two weeks for an in-person screening.

Ready to accelerate your AI career and join a fast-growing startup? Apply now and help build the future of intelligent systems.

Job Types: Full-time, Internship

Pay: $20.38 - $21.84 per hour

Work Location: In person

The ideal candidate is eager to grow, has foundational technical skills, and thrives in a collaborative, fast-paced environment.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Assist in designing, building, and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines to process data from various sources.
Support data integration by cleaning, transforming, and validating data to ensure accuracy and consistency.
Help identify and troubleshoot basic performance issues in data pipelines and queries, escalating complex problems to senior team members.
Contribute to the development and maintenance of data models and schemas to meet business and analytical needs.
Collaborate with analysts and stakeholders to understand data requirements and assist in delivering solutions.
Participate in monitoring and maintaining data pipelines, ensuring system reliability with guidance from senior engineers.
Document data processes, pipelines, and workflows to maintain clear and accurate records.
Engage in team code reviews and adhere to data engineering best practices under supervision.

QUALIFICATIONS:

Education: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, Data Science, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
Technical Skills:

o Basic proficiency in programming languages such as Python.

o Fundamental knowledge of SQL for querying and basic data manipulation.

o Familiarity with relational databases (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL).

o Exposure to cloud platforms such as Azure (e.g., Fabric, Synapse, Data Lake) is a plus.

o Basic understanding of data orchestration tools (e.g., Apache Airflow) is a plus.

o Awareness of data warehousing or data lake concepts is a plus.

Soft Skills:

o Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

o Effective communication skills to collaborate with team members.

o Ability to learn quickly and adapt to new tools and processes.

o Team-oriented mindset with a willingness to take direction and feedback.

Experience: 0–2 years of experience in data engineering, including internships, academic projects, or relevant coursework.

OCI BENEFITS

OCI is committed to investing in our associates to help them unleash their potential. We offer competitive salaries, a 401(K)-matching program, and a wide selection of health and wellness benefits. These benefits also include wellness activities, parking allowances, access to continuous learning, paid holidays and a generous vacation package, workplace giving, free access to financial resources, gym membership discounts, and more!

COMPANY CULTURE:

Our associates work collectively toward our vision. We share a set of truths referred to as P.A.C.E!

PEOPLE first. Everything else follows. We work hard to develop respectful, collaborative relationships and meet shared goals.
ACTION. We are passionate entrepreneurs and execute with a sense of urgency and strong will to win. We fuel creativity and innovation.
CURIOSITY. We have a deep desire to learn and grow.
ETHICS. We do the right thing in our business and community, even when no one is looking.
! We have fun and make our mark! We get excited about what we do, we enjoy the process and recognize that enthusiasm and engagement are contagious.

About Focuz

Focuz is a well-funded early-stage startup on a mission to redefine video intelligence. We are building a next-generation platform that transforms raw video streams from any camera into structured, actionable insights. Our architecture is designed to be robust, scalable, and portable, capable of running both in the cloud and on-premise for our customers. We are a small, agile team of builders, and we're looking for foundational members who share our passion for solving hard problems in distributed systems and networking.

About the Role
As our foundational Senior DevOps Engineer, you will be the architect of our entire infrastructure, automation, and networking strategy. This is a high-impact role that goes far beyond traditional CI/CD and cloud management. Your primary mission will be to solve the complex networking challenges inherent in connecting thousands of on-premise devices to our cloud services, and to wrap those solutions in elegant, code-driven automation. You will be responsible for everything from deploying our core services to architecting the network pathways that allow real-time video to flow reliably across firewalls and private networks. You will own our "Infrastructure as Code" vision, our observability platform, and the deployment strategy for our on-site agents. If you are excited by the challenge of building a resilient, distributed system and are not afraid to dive deep into networking protocols, this role is for you.


What You'll Do
Lead our "Infrastructure as Code" (IaC) strategy from the ground up. You will write the code (using tools like Pulumi or Terraform) that defines and manages all our cloud and on-premise resources.
Design and automate the deployment of critical networking infrastructure, including STUN/TURN servers for WebRTC NAT traversal and highly-available MQTT brokers for IoT communication.
Solve real-world network complexity problems to ensure reliable connectivity between our on-premise agents and our cloud platform.
Architect and implement a comprehensive observability platform. You will be responsible for gathering and correlating metrics, logs, and traces from every part of our system - from on-site camera stream health and network bandwidth to our cloud APIs and AI workflows.
Develop and maintain robust CI/CD pipelines (e.g., using GitHub Actions) to automate the entire build, test, and deployment lifecycle.
Own the containerization strategy for all our applications, creating efficient and secure Docker images designed to run on customer-managed hardware.
Collaborate closely with the development team to diagnose connectivity issues, optimize data flow, and build a seamless, reliable developer experience.
What you'll bring
5+ years of professional experience in a DevOps, SRE, or Infrastructure Engineering role with a strong software engineering mindset.
Deep, hands-on expertise with Infrastructure as Code (IaC). You must have significant experience with modern IaC tools like Pulumi (highly preferred) or Terraform.
A strong, practical understanding of networking principles and protocols (TCP/IP, UDP, DNS, firewalls, NAT). You must be able to reason about and solve complex connectivity issues.
Expert-level knowledge of Docker and containerization. You should be comfortable deploying and managing Docker applications on bare-metal Linux servers.
Proven experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines for a production environment.
A strong passion for and practical experience with observability. You should have experience building monitoring and logging solutions (e.g., using the Prometheus/Grafana stack).
Experience with a major cloud provider (AWS, GCP, or Azure).
A proactive, ownership-driven mindset and the ability to drive complex technical projects from concept to completion.
Nice to have
Direct experience deploying and managing real-time communication infrastructure (STUN/TURN, MQTT, WebRTC gateways) is a massive advantage.
Proficiency in a general-purpose programming language for infrastructure tasks (e.g., Python, Go, TypeScript), especially in the context of a tool like Pulumi.
Experience with configuration management tools like Ansible.
Familiarity with GitOps principles and tools (e.g., ArgoCD, Flux).

What we offer
A fully remote and flexible work environment.
A foundational role with a massive impact on the product, architecture, and company culture.
The opportunity to solve challenging networking and infrastructure problems that are core to our business.
A key strategic position where you will build a modern, greenfield infrastructure with a "code-first" philosophy.

About InMarket

Since 2010, InMarket has been the leader in 360-degree consumer intelligence and real-time activation for thousands of today's top brands. Through InMarket's data-driven marketing platform, brands can build targeted audiences, activate media in real time, and measure success in driving return on ad spend. InMarket's proprietary Moments offering outperforms traditional mobile advertising by 6x.* Our LCI attribution platform, which won the MarTech Breakthrough Award for Best Advertising Measurement Platform, was validated by Forrester to drive an average of $40 ROAS for our clients.

Source: Wordstream US Google Display Benchmarks for Mobile Media



About the role

The Data Insights Analyst will have direct access to the world's most advanced 1st and 3rd party data sets. They will use statistical tools and direct SQL queries to mine anonymous consumer behavior data to understand who, how, and why people make purchase decisions. They will create actionable insights for our Fortune 500 clients, public reports, and financial partners. Keen creative and intelligence skills are required to find game changing insights in big data and weave the fact-based persuasive story together in a way non-data junky business people can digest and take action on.

Your daily impact as a Data Insights Analyst

Partner with Marketing, Client Strategy, Sales, AdOps and Campaign Insights Managers to deliver client and market insights
Construct useful frameworks and analyze visit data, purchase history demographics and more to learn strategic insights
Interpret data and translate complex concepts to external-facing stories in a clear and concise manner
Build new insights from idea generation through development and production
Determine patterns and trends that can be communicated externally
Provide custom analysis and data visualization builds for internal and external stakeholders as needed
Collaborate with Data Science, Product and Tech to deliver novel solutions for large, open-ended requests
Set the bar for unique, actionable and insightful data

Your experience and expertise

Bachelor's degree preferably in Statistics or related quantitative field (e.g. Computer Science, Economics, Mathematics, Physics, STEM, etc.)
Advanced proficiency in Excel
SQL expert
Experience with databases such as MySQL, Redshift, PostgreSql, BigQuery
Ability to answer business questions from our vast existing data sets
Strong data analysis, communication and presentation skills

Company Matched 401(k)

Talented co-workers and management

SafeLease is an insurance technology company that specializes in commercial real estate.

We focus on insurance products for the self-storage industry but are rapidly expanding our suite of services. We believe traditional commercial insurance can be distributed faster and cheaper. To do that, we’ve brought the entire insurance value chain—distribution, underwriting, and reinsurance—under one roof to drive innovation in an industry that’s ripe for change.

We’re hiring a Senior Data Analyst, who will be responsible for analyzing and interpreting data related to our commercial real estate products and services. This position will work closely with cross-functional teams, including product development, finance, and marketing, to identify opportunities for revenue growth, pricing optimization, and process automation.

About The Role

Your data-driven insights will inform decision-making and play a vital role in achieving our goal of maximizing the value of commercial real estate assets.

To drive our mission forward, this role will:

Develop, implement, and maintain advanced analytics systems that simplify complex data problems into actionable insights.

Analyze complex datasets to identify trends, opportunities for growth, and areas for improvement.

Analyze data from projects, updates management with progress and makes recommendations based upon team's findings.

Create comprehensive reports based on data mining, analysis, and visualization to support business decisions.

Build pipelines for data cleaning & data transformation.

Communicate with multiple teams to understand their analytical needs.

Perform analysis in excel through formulas, v-lookup and pivot tables.

Liaise with internal stakeholders to build collaboration and ensure operational activities align with business goals and strategies.

About You

Strong analytic abilities, expertise with data visualization tools, and excellent cross-functional communication skills will be the key to success in this role.

You’ll bring these skills and strengths:

Demonstrated experience delivering solutions in any of the following platform-based cloud solutions; AWS, Microsoft Azure, Snowflake, Databricks, or Google Cloud.

Project based experience applying predictive and descriptive analytics.

About Alpha Recon

Alpha Recon is a rapidly growing SaaS company that is disrupting the risk technology and security management space. We will be helping to save lives.

Alpha Recon strives to be the most trusted threat and risk intelligence source in the security world. We provide real-time, valuable, and actionable insights gained by integrating elusive data inputs, proprietary analysis, expert human intellect, artificial intelligence & machine learning, and predictive analytics.

The candidate must be entrepreneurial, motivated, well organized and willing to work for stock options initially! Company milestones will trigger salary, but salary will be zero to start. Please don't apply if you require immediate salary. There are strong business opportunities already in place and we will offer salary based on agreed upon company milestones. We prefer entrepreneurial minded candidates that wish to be with the company long term.

About the Opportunity

We are seeking a highly experienced and visionary Full stack Architect to join our dynamic engineering team. This role is pivotal in designing, developing, and evolving scalable, high-performance, and secure software solutions across our entire technology stack. The Full stack Architect will drive technical excellence, provide architectural guidance, and mentor development teams, ensuring the successful delivery of complex projects from concept to deployment. This position requires a deep understanding of both front-end and back-end technologies, cloud-native architectures, and a passion for building robust, innovative applications.

Responsibilities

Architectural Design & Strategy:

Lead the design and implementation of end-to-end architectural solutions for complex web applications and services.
Define technical standards, best practices, and architectural patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures).
Translate business requirements into scalable and resilient technical designs.
Conduct architectural reviews and ensure adherence to established standards across all development initiatives.

Technical Leadership & Mentorship:

Provide technical leadership and guidance to multiple development teams across the full stack.
Mentor junior and senior engineers, fostering a culture of continuous learning and technical growth.
Drive innovation by researching and evaluating new technologies, tools, and frameworks.

Full stack Development:

Actively participate in hands-on development, writing high-quality, maintainable, and well-tested code for both front-end and back-end components.
Oversee the integration of various systems and third-party services.
Ensure optimal performance, scalability, and security of applications.

Collaboration & Communication:

Collaborate closely with product managers, UX/UI designers, DevOps, and other stakeholders to align technical solutions with business goals.
Communicate complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Lead technical discussions and decision-making processes.

Quality & Reliability:

Implement robust testing strategies, including unit, integration, and end-to-end testing.
Ensure high availability, disaster recovery, and operational excellence for all deployed systems.
Identify and resolve complex technical issues and performance bottlenecks.

Qualifications

Education: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
Experience: 5 to 8 years of progressive experience in software development, with at least 2 -3 years in an Architect role focusing on full stack solutions.
Proven track record of designing and delivering large-scale, distributed systems.

Technical Skills (Deep Expertise in at least one from each category, strong familiarity with others):

Front-End: React, Angular, Vue.js, Flutter or similar modern JavaScript frameworks. Strong command of HTML5, CSS3, and responsive design principles.
Back-End: Node.js, Python (Django/Flask), Java (Spring Boot), Go, or similar.
Mobile Development: Cross-platform frameworks (React Native, Flutter).
Databases: Relational (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server) and NoSQL (MongoDB, Cassandra, Redis, DynamoDB).
Cloud Platforms: MS Azure, with experience in serverless, containers (Docker, Kubernetes), and managed services.
APIs & Integration: RESTful APIs, GraphQL, microservices architecture, event-driven patterns (Kafka, RabbitMQ).
DevOps & CI/CD: Git, Jenkins, GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, or similar tools.
Architectural Principles: Strong understanding of software design patterns, architectural styles, and principles of scalability, reliability, and security.
Problem-Solving: Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to tackle complex technical challenges.
Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to articulate technical concepts to diverse audiences.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience with AI/ML integration into full stack applications.
Familiarity with data streaming technologies and real-time analytics.
Contributions to open-source projects or active participation in the developer community.

The Role

The Manager, DevOps Engineering is responsible for supporting, evolving, and maintaining high-performing, highly available cloud-native systems at scale. This role drives the adoption of modern DevOps practices, ensures infrastructure scalability and resilience, and empowers development teams through the use of common industry frameworks. The manager leads a team of engineers focused on enhancing system performance, reducing operational overhead, and ensuring secure, compliant deployment pipelines.

Employee Value Proposition

As a pivotal member of a tight-knit, collaborative team, you'll play a vital role in building and scaling a new, innovative platform. We're exploring emerging technologies and transitioning toward self-sufficient, agile systems. Your influence will shape the way our developers build, test, and release software.

Key Performance Objectives

In the first 12–18 months, success will be measured by your ability to:

Implement a self-service DevOps platform that allows developers to deploy, test, and monitor applications independently.
Drive continuous delivery adoption, including infrastructure as code, automated release orchestration, and real-time performance feedback loops.
Enhance system observability and resilience by leveraging advanced monitoring tools (e.g., New Relic, Dynatrace) and automated alerting for production systems.
Foster strong cross-functional collaboration with Development, QA, Product, and Security teams to align on delivery goals and operational excellence.

Primary Responsibilities & Deliverables

DevOps Strategy & Execution

Lead the end-to-end design and continuous improvement of DevOps and SRE practices.
Own CI/CD systems, cloud-native infrastructure, and deployment strategies across environments.

Team Leadership & Delivery Management

Mentor and develop a high-performing team of DevOps engineers.
Allocate capacity between support, project work, automation initiatives, and incident response.

Platform & Infrastructure Ownership

Continue current adoption of infrastructure as code (Terraform/CloudFormation) and container orchestration (Kubernetes, Docker).

Cross-Team Collaboration

Act as a key partner for Development, QA, and Security teams to streamline release cycles and eliminate delivery bottlenecks.
Participate in technical architecture reviews and post-mortems to ensure the production readiness of all systems.

Success Profile (Qualifications & Experience)

Education & Leadership

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related discipline.
5+ years in DevOps or SRE roles, with 3+ years of direct people leadership.

Technical Expertise

Mastery of cloud platforms (preferably AWS) and orchestration (Kubernetes).
Experience with infrastructure tools (Terraform, CloudFormation).
Skilled in CI/CD systems (Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD, CircleCI) and monitoring tools (New Relic, Dynatrace).
Strong knowledge of configuration management tools (Ansible, Puppet, or Chef).
Experience with API Gateway platforms (AWS API Gateway, Apigee).

Performance & Metrics-Driven

Proven ability to use telemetry (logs, traces, metrics) to diagnose and improve system performance.
Deep understanding of modern deployment strategies, including canary, blue/green, and rolling deployments.

Work Environment

Willingness to participate in off-hours support rotations, including evenings, weekends, and holidays when needed.
Committed to continuous improvement, ethical delivery, and maintaining strong stakeholder relationships.

Founded in gut health. Experts in microbiome. Plexus Worldwide®, headquartered in Scottsdale, Arizona, is a renowned direct-selling company recognized as a top employer. Over the past 17 years, Plexus has dedicated itself to promoting hope, health, and happiness through its scientifically backed, innovative dietary supplements, weight management products, and personal care products, sold primarily through Plexus Brand Ambassadors. As a six-time Best Places to Work awardee, Plexus prides itself on its robust organizational culture and commitment to community support and philanthropy. Our team is driven by a shared mission to empower individuals to achieve their health and wellness goals while offering additional income through the sale of products. Company Description


AbbVie's mission is to discover and deliver innovative medicines and solutions that solve serious health issues today and address the medical challenges of tomorrow. We strive to have a remarkable impact on people's lives across several key therapeutic areas – immunology, oncology, neuroscience, and eye care – and products and services in our Allergan Aesthetics portfolio. For more information about AbbVie, please visit us at www.abbvie.com. Follow @abbvie on X, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, LinkedIn and Tik Tok.


Job Description


Purpose

The Senior Director reports to the Vice President of R&D Data & AI Convergence.
AbbVie-wide multidisciplinary scientific expert and key opinion leader responsible for developing and driving the scientific and strategic objectives of the Convergence organization.


Responsibilities

As the scientific leader for the Convergence organization, responsible for developing and executing strategies to generate and deploy novel analysis of large, cross-functional, internal and external datasets in a way that advances the objectives of the entire AbbVie R&D community and positions AbbVie as an industry-leading knowledge-based biopharmaceutical company.
Establishes relationships with leaders across R&D functions to develop a broader understanding of organizational needs and opportunities, enabling alignment between the Convergence scientific strategy and other functional strategies.
Partners with senior R&D leaders to ensure that the Convergence scientific strategy is in alignment with and supportive of functional objectives and is advancing AbbVie’s strategic priorities.
Leads the cross-functional team of R&D Data & Digital Catalysts, both supporting their work and ensuring that their work is in alignment with the larger Convergence scientific strategy.
Anticipates challenges and continually sets approaches to utilize newly proven technology. Anticipates and prioritizes emerging scientific and technology trends. Champions global use of innovation sourced internally or externally.
Partners with Information Research (IR) to drive technology and capability builds that enable assembly of large data sets and advanced analytics.
Leads the exchange of scientific knowledge across functions by leveraging the Convergence ecosystem along with other communication channels and proactively advising, sharing knowledge and expert opinions.
Establishes scientific standards that position AbbVie as a global industry leader. Leads and champions changes in standards through the senior leadership team.
Partner with BTS Partnership leaders to establishes relationships with external scientific leaders and supports the exchange of expert opinions and visions of technology.
Develops industry reputation as a key opinion leader. Participates on panel discussions at international meetings and on the standards committee for industry consortium/trade groups.
Mentors more junior scientists in alignment with the Convergence scientific strategy.

Qualifications

PhD in scientific discipline with at least 14 years of relevant work experience; Master’s degree in scientific discipline with at least 20 years of relevant work experience; or Bachelor’s degree in scientific discipline and at least 22 years of relevant work experience.
Proven ability to innovate with robust history of publication.
Ability to advise and influence discipline experts and leaders across AbbVie.
Interacts well with diverse groups across functions and maintains strong working relationships with internal and external collaborators.
Listens and understands others’ point of views and articulates tactfully and respectfully one’s own perspective orally, in writing and in presentations.
Interpersonal skills capable of negotiating and reconciling interdisciplinary differences, optimizing AbbVie goals.

Bitly is the world's leading Connections Platform, empowering businesses to create memorable connections with their customers at every physical and digital touchpoint. From Fortune 500 companies to small businesses, millions of people around the world rely on Bitly for custom short links, QR Codes, and landing pages that are easy to create, share, and manage. With tools to track and optimize every interaction, we help businesses understand what drives the greatest impact.

As we continue to redefine how brands interact with customers, we're looking for passionate individuals eager to drive innovation and shape the future of engagement. Join us in turning every click, tap, and scan into a powerful catalyst for connections.

We embrace diversity in experience and background as a vital strength. If you're passionate and eager to make an impact, we invite you to apply—even if you don't meet all the requirements. Different perspectives lead to the best ideas, and we'd love to hear yours!

The Role

Bitly is looking for a Staff Backend Engineer to develop, improve and expand the services that power all of our products. You will be focused primarily on adding to and improving our feature set while contributing to ongoing efforts around codebase modernization.

You will work closely with the engineering and product teams aligned within distinct areas of ownership. Your team will most likely be named after an animal.

What You Will Do

Drive technical excellence across teams by mentoring Senior and Mid-level Engineers, fostering a culture of quality, scalability, and innovation.
Advocate for engineering solutions that align with product strategy and measurable business outcomes, influencing roadmaps and cross-functional priorities.
Create, manage, and improve back end services that accurately and efficiently provide clients with the data they need with a variety of tools and languages including, Go, NSQ, MySQL, Memcached and more.
Lead cross-functional engineering initiatives to evolve platform architecture, influence technical direction company-wide, and align distributed teams on scalable systems design.
Translate deep systems knowledge into high-impact architectural decisions and frameworks that enable rapid development and long-term maintainability.
Leverage AI-powered developer tools (e.g., Codex, Cursor, Copilot) to boost team productivity and ensure best practices in code generation, review, and testing.

Who You Are

You have extensive engineering experience with the ability to design robust and scalable services.
You have expertise in one back end language with experience in others.
You have experience mentoring other engineers and a desire to continue doing so.
You have a solid background in writing understandable, testable, and efficient code in a language such as Golang, Python, Java, C# or Node.js.
You stay current with emerging AI technologies and thoughtfully evaluate their applicability to back end systems, engineering workflows, and company-wide initiatives.
You clearly communicate technical concepts and ideas to other engineers and other parts of the organization.
You adapt easily to changing priorities, team structures, and technical directions, maintaining momentum and clarity even in evolving contexts.
You take the initiative in addressing issues or seeking support from fellow team members.
You have experience with and are a proponent of automated testing.
You have experience using a flavor of version control—we use git and Github to collaborate and manage pull requests.

Bonus Points:

Extensive experience with GoLang.
Experience with AI-powered development tools
Experience writing and maintaining APIs.
Experience with API migrations.
Experience with Test Driven Development

US Employee Benefits

Your benefits start on Day 1!

Health & Wellbeing

Inclusive health, dental, and vision plans built to support diverse lifestyles
Employer contribution to HSA plans
Generous paid parental leave
Enhanced support for reproductive health, family planning, and new parents
Robust mental health support and Employee Assistance Program (EAP) with confidential counseling services
Comprehensive well-being benefits including reimbursement program
Flexible PTO policy and company breaks - At Bitly, we believe rest and relaxation are essential for rejuvenating the creative spirit that sets us apart

Day to day as a Business Intelligence Analyst II

As a full-time Business Intelligence Analyst II at RTO National, you will play a crucial role in supporting the implementation of strategic initiatives through in-depth data analysis of key performance indicators (KPIs). Your expertise in SQL, Power BI, and analytics will enable you to analyze large, complex datasets, leveraging data mining and statistical techniques to identify significant trends and patterns that inform decision-making. You will develop actionable recommendations and partner and present your findings in a clear, concise manner to business leaders. Additionally, you will create comprehensive reports and dashboards to streamline business processes and conduct various ad-hoc analyses to drive performance improvements. Your ability to gather and document information effectively will support departmental operations, ensuring all deliverables are met in a timely fashion while consulting stakeholders on enhancing performance metric reporting.

This role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to the core business strategies that shape RTO National's success in the finance industry.

Are you the Business Intelligence Analyst II we're looking for?

To be successful in this role at RTO National, you will need a robust skill set that combines technical proficiency and analytical acumen. A strong command of SQL is essential for database querying and data preparation, allowing you to navigate complex datasets efficiently. Your role will demand critical thinking and problem-solving skills, enabling you to interpret data trends and provide insights that drive strategic initiatives. Embracing a customer-centric approach and exhibiting creativity will further empower you to design tailored solutions that address business challenges.

In this high-performance environment, accountability and respect for differing skill sets will be key to your success and growth.

Experienced user in Power BI

column10:
Cribl does differently.

What does that mean? It means we are a serious company that doesn't take itself too seriously; and we're looking for people who love to get stuff done, and laugh a bit along the way. We're growing rapidly - looking for collaborative, curious, and motivated team members who are passionate about putting customers first. As a remote-first company we believe in empowering our employees to do their best work, wherever they are.

As the data engine for IT and Security many of the biggest names in the most demanding industries trust Cribl to solve their most pressing data needs. Ready to do the best work of your career? Join the herd and unlock your opportunity.

Why You'll Love This Role

Are you someone who is excited about enabling customers to find, analyze, and visualize their operational data, no matter where it lives, regardless of format or storage medium? Do you enjoy query languages, optimization of performance and working on cutting edge technologies?

Cribl Search flips observability on its head by distributing queries to where your data already lives and aggregating the results - no more expensive data replication, centralized aggregation, megaIOPS+petabyte storage, or kilocore+terabyte hosts required to see what's going on in your deployed environments. Want to find specific events, trace an execution, identify a threat, or graph metric trends from (ex.) data stored in EBS volumes, Cribl Edge nodes, S3 buckets, Azure blob stores, containers, RDBMSes, data lakes, or even a custom datastore? How about being able to do that from a single pane, regardless of heterogeneous storage / location / hosting / on-disk format? That's what Cribl Search enables, and it is a game-changer for helping Cribl become the data engine for IT and security.

Cribl Inc is looking for a backend focused Mid-level Software Engineer to collaborate on a remote-first, diverse, inclusive, cross-functional team of software developers focused on expanding the integration of Cribl Search with 3rd party providers. In this role, you will have the opportunity to continue to shape the future of Cribl Search by expanding development on a novel distributed and federated search system for in-place data. This is an ideal role for anyone who is passionate about building high-performance applications in a dynamic and collaborative environment.

On top of it all, you will have fun! Cribl strives to be a great place to work for everyone.


As An Active Member Of Our Team, You Will...

Write clean, maintainable, and testable code with an eye towards observability and resilience
Collaborate with peers and cross-functional team members as you contribute to meeting project goals and acceptance criteria
Work to meet and refine the software development practices that make our team effective — everybody's feedback helps shape our process
Work on a mix of tech debt, bug fixes, and new features
Participate in the entire software development lifecycle from requirements and design to testing, deployment, and production support/monitoring
Share on-call and take part in team that embraces a model of service ownership
This position will require stand-by, on-call, or off-hours duties




If You've Got It - We Want It

You are a self starter, require minimal supervision and have a track record of learning quickly
Ability to problem solve from first principles and maintain focus on overarching objectives
Experience with data structures and algorithms
Experience with agile processes and rapid iterative development
Experience in developing distributed systems and working with data at scale
Experience working in a dynamic and highly-innovative environment
Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written; able to explain complex technical topics to both internal and external stakeholders with ease and in remote/distributed environments
Experience in integrating with 3rd party APIs
Familiar with AWS, Lambda and Cloud Infrastructure
Experience building search, query and/or analytic systems working with structured and unstructured data
Experience with frontend development is a bonus, as 5-10% of our work needs frontend contributions too
No fear! Speak your mind
Good jokes, or maybe better, bad jokes
A love for goats
Experience working remotely
Experience with Typescript/JavaScript, NodeJS, and SQL




Salary Range ($145,000 - $175,000)

The salary for this role is dependent on geographic location. The salary offered within the range described will be based on the individual candidate's job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. In addition to a competitive salary, Cribl also offers a generous benefits package which includes health, dental, vision, short-term disability, and life insurance, paid holidays and paid time off, a fertility treatment benefit, 401(k), equity, and eligibility for a discretionary company-wide bonus.

Who We Are

Imprint is reimagining co-branded credit cards & financial products to be smarter, more rewarding, and truly brand-first. We partner with companies like H-E-B, Turkish Airlines, Brooks Brothers, and Eddie Bauer to launch modern credit programs that deepen loyalty, unlock savings, and drive growth. Our platform combines advanced payments infrastructure, intelligent underwriting, and seamless UX to help brands offer powerful financial products—without becoming a bank.

Co-branded cards account for over $300 billion in U.S. annual spend—but most are still powered by legacy banks. Imprint is the modern alternative: flexible, tech-forward, and built for today’s consumer. Backed by Kleiner Perkins, Thrive Capital, and Khosla Ventures, we’re building a world-class team to redefine how people pay—and how brands grow. If you want to work fast, solve hard problems, and make a real impact, we’d love to meet you.

The Team

The Data Engineering team at Imprint is responsible for building and scaling the data infrastructure that supports product development, analytics, operations, and machine learning across the company. We own the pipelines, platforms, and processes that empower our stakeholders to trust and act on our data.

The Role

As our Staff Data Engineer, you'll architect our data platform while solving our most complex technical challenges. You'll build the foundation for Imprint's next decade of growth: scaling infrastructure for explosive expansion, delivering insights that drive million-dollar decisions, enabling bulletproof partner data sharing, and transforming how every team leverages data. Join us in creating a platform that doesn't just meet today's needs—but anticipates tomorrow's possibilities.

What You’ll Do
Build & Scale Infrastructure

Architect our next-generation data platform, optimizing Snowflake, dbt Cloud, and real-time CDC pipelines for enterprise scale

Design secure, compliant partner data delivery systems via Snowflake shares, S3/SFTP integrations, and Marketplace listings

Create mission-critical financial reporting pipelines with exceptional accuracy and reliability

Drive Technical Excellence

Establish company-wide data standards for modeling, lineage, contracts, and orchestration

Champion data reliability, observability, and trust across all systems

Make strategic technology decisions that balance innovation with pragmatism

Lead & Mentor

Elevate engineering practices across Analytics, Data, and Engineering teams

Conduct architecture reviews and build reusable frameworks

Share expertise through documentation, templates, and hands-on guidance

What We Look For

10+ years of experience in data engineering or related fields, with proven experience owning platform-level architecture and strategy.

Deep expertise in Snowflake, dbt Cloud, Change Data Capture frameworks, orchestration tools (Airflow, dbt Cloud), and reverse ETL.

Strong background in external data sharing and partner integrations, including Snowflake data shares, S3/SFTP pipelines, and Marketplace listings.

Proven ability to design and implement data governance and observability systems, including data contracts, lineage tracking, anomaly detection, and automated monitoring.

Strong engineering skills in SQL and Python, emphasizing testing, CI/CD, and maintainability in complex data systems.

Reputation as a mentor and technical authority who elevates the technical quality and rigor of peers and teams.

Exceptional communication skills and the ability to influence technical and business leadership across multiple departments.

Perks & Benefits

Competitive compensation and equity packages

Leading configured work computers of your choice

Flexible paid time off

Fully covered, high-quality healthcare, including fully covered dependent coverage

Additional health coverage includes access to One Medical and the option to enroll in an FSA

16 weeks of paid parental leave for the primary caregiver and 8 weeks for all new parents

An understanding that successful hybrid work requires flexibility and an appreciation for asynchronous work

Access to industry-leading technology across all of our business units, stemming from our philosophy that we should invest in resources for our team that foster innovation, optimization, and productivity

Imprint is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace. Imprint is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status. Imprint welcomes talented individuals from all backgrounds who want to build the future of payments and rewards. If you are passionate about FinTech and eager to grow, let’s move the world forward, together.

Compensation Range: $200K - $250K

We are seeking an experienced and motivated Data Engineer with a minimum of 3 years of experience to join our high-performing team. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on solving complex data challenges, designing robust integration workflows, and securing high-volume data migrations. Are you passionate about building scalable data pipelines and modernizing enterprise data ecosystems? Do you have experience working with Talend, Qlik Replicate, and cloud technologies like Azure or AWS? If so, this opportunity could be the next step in your career.

Primary Role: The Data Engineer will support efforts to architect, develop, and maintain large-scale data migration and integration pipelines. You will be responsible for building secure, efficient, and automated ETL/ELT processes that support the movement of both structured and unstructured data across on-premise and cloud environments. Your role will be central to supporting mission-critical data workflows and enabling digital transformation initiatives.

How You Drive Value: Your expertise in data migration, cloud platforms, and ETL development will optimize data architecture, reduce latency, and enhance data integrity. Your contributions will ensure the secure, accurate, and timely delivery of data across the organization and help shape our customer's next-generation data capabilities.

You will:

Collaborate with data architects, project managers, and business stakeholders to design and execute comprehensive data migration strategies for structured (e.g., relational databases) and unstructured (e.g., documents, media) data.
Use Qlik Replicate to implement real-time data replication and Change Data Capture (CDC) processes from various source systems to modern cloud platforms.
Design, implement, and optimize Talend ETL/ELT pipelines for transforming and integrating large datasets across multiple systems.
Ensure seamless integration of data from diverse sources (on-premises databases, cloud environments, ADLS, etc.) into platforms such as Azure, AWS, and Microsoft Dataverse.
Automate recurring data tasks including validation, transformation, and reconciliation to maintain accuracy and streamline workflows.
Conduct data profiling, cleansing, mapping, and transformation to ensure data quality and consistency throughout migration and integration.
Perform thorough testing—including unit, integration, and performance testing—to validate the completeness and accuracy of all migrated data.
Troubleshoot issues, analyze root causes, and optimize performance during and after data migration.
Develop and maintain clear, comprehensive documentation for data workflows, scripts, configurations, and process standards.
Provide timely status updates to stakeholders, resolve data-related challenges, and ensure alignment with business requirements.
Stay informed of emerging tools and best practices in data engineering, migration, and cloud technologies to continuously enhance system performance.

You Have:

Must be a United States Citizen.
Must possess and maintain Department of Defense (DoD) Secret Level Security Clearance.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in data engineering, with a focus on enterprise-scale data migration and integration projects.
Proven hands-on experience with Talend for ETL/ELT development and Qlik Replicate for real-time replication and CDC.
Strong background working with both structured data (e.g., SQL-based systems, data warehouses) and unstructured data (e.g., files, NoSQL, multimedia).
Experience deploying solutions on cloud platforms such as Azure or AWS for data storage, migration, and processing.
Solid understanding of data modeling, architecture, governance, and best practices for large-scale data solutions.
Proficiency with SQL and NoSQL databases and working knowledge of diverse integration tools and frameworks.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with a focus on optimizing system performance and data integrity.
Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, for effective collaboration across technical and non-technical teams.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality results in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.

We Would Love it if You Had:

Experience with modern data lake and warehouse architectures (e.g., Snowflake, Redshift, Azure Synapse).
Familiarity with infrastructure-as-code and automation tools for managing cloud-based data services.
A background working in regulated environments with a strong focus on data security, compliance, and auditing.

This position offers a unique opportunity to work on forward-leaning data transformation projects while optimizing the performance and security of mission-critical workflows. If you are ready to take the next step in your data engineering career and thrive in a collaborative, innovation-driven environment, we encourage you to apply.

Want to hear more? Read on to see what our Team Members say about their experience:

column10:
Company Description


Resultant is an outcomes-focused consulting firm committed to helping clients make technology a strategic asset and use data to guide better decisions. But we’re not just data and tech experts; we are problem solvers and industry experts who work alongside our clients to help them achieve their mission.

We don’t solve problems for our clients. We solve problems with them.

We take the time to deeply understand goals and roadblocks to drive toward outcomes that serve organizations, people, and communities. Through outcomes driven by data analytics, technology solutions, digital transformation, and beyond, our team works with clients in both the public and private sectors to solve their most complex challenges. We start by learning as much as we can about who they are, how they work, and what they’re striving for so we can feel their problems as our own. Partnering with our clients means their desired outcomes are always top of mind, their challenges and strengths guiding our efforts. We build client-focused relationships before we build unique solutions that blaze past expectations.

Originally founded in Indianapolis in 2008, Resultant now employs more than 450 team members who operate remotely and from offices and hubs around the United States including Indianapolis, IN; Fort Wayne, IN; Denver, CO; Atlanta, GA; and Dallas, TX.

We’re Resultant. Clients partner with us to see a difference. People join us to make one.


Job Description


Resultant is seeking a Cloud and Systems Architect to join our Managed Services Projects team. If you are someone who enjoys working directly with stakeholders at various levels of an organization, this is the position for you! An ideal candidate deeply understands how to balance the moving parts associated with managing on-premise and cloud-native software implementations, has experience helping clients define business requirements, and quickly turns those requirements into action.

We are ideally seeking candidates based in either the Indianapolis, IN or Dallas, TX areas. Travel may be required 25%–50% of the time, depending on proximity to clients.

The position will work with public and private sector clients to ensure that appropriate foundations, frameworks, security, structures, and governance are available, and applied for Enterprise Application Services.

Consider your day-to-day responsibilities in this role:

Lead end-to-end modernization of legacy applications and endpoints, migrating from on-premise to cloud-native environments using Azure, VMware, M365, and Intune
Develop migration roadmaps, assess application and infrastructure readiness, and ensure minimal disruption to business operations
Architect and implement multi-cloud and hybrid cloud strategies, with a focus on secure, scalable transitions to Azure, GCP, or AWS
Evaluate and select cloud and datacenter services to meet requirements for scalability, security, and cost-efficiency
Design and advise on hybrid networking architectures, including high-level planning for routing, firewalls, VPNs, and private connectivity between datacenter and cloud environments
Define and guide hybrid data architecture strategies, including SAN/NAS integration, Azure Files, Blob Storage, S3 buckets, and cross-platform access planning for structured and unstructured data
Assess and optimize existing infrastructure and deployment methodologies to align with cloud adoption frameworks and business goals
Design and document scalable, secure, and reliable system architectures, including visual diagrams, technical documentation, and implementation plans
Architect and oversee VMware environments, including vSphere, VMFS, storage provisioning, and integration with cloud platforms
Implement best practices for hybrid software deployment and endpoint management, leveraging Intune, Azure, and M365 for compliance and lifecycle management
Collaborate across service desk, engineering, security, and business teams to define and iterate on modernization strategies
Provide technical leadership on cloud architecture, governance, and adoption, translating business needs into robust technical solutions
Develop and maintain cloud governance frameworks, including identity, access control, data protection, and resource usage policies
Conduct cloud environment assessments to identify opportunities for performance optimization, cost savings, and security enhancements
Lead automation initiatives using Infrastructure-as-Code tools like Terraform and Azure DevOps, promoting CI/CD and DevOps best practices
Mentor and coach team members on cloud technologies, VMware architecture, hybrid networking, and data strategies, fostering a culture of technical excellence
Support pre-sales and solution scoping, including workshops, requirements gathering, and SOW creation
Troubleshoot and resolve complex issues across cloud infrastructure, networking, identity, and VMware environments, ensuring high availability
Stay current with trends in cloud computing, identity, networking, and hybrid data platforms, and proactively recommend improvements

Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, Engineering, or related field; Master’s preferred
10+ years of experience in IT architecture, cloud computing, or related technical roles
Deep expertise in Azure, VMware, Datacenter and at least one other major cloud platform (GCP or AWS)
Extensive experience with Microsoft 365 (Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, OneDrive) and Intune, including integration, migration, automation, and governance.
Strong experience in designing and advising on hybrid networking architectures, including secure connectivity between datacenter and cloud environments (routing, firewalls, VPNs, express route, private links)
Solid understanding and experience with hybrid data architectures, including SAN/NAS storage, S3 buckets, Azure Files, Blob Storage, and cross-platform data access strategies
Proven track record in cloud migration, hybrid architecture, and aligning infrastructure with business and compliance requirements
Background in infrastructure and network architecture, including virtualization, identity, and endpoint management
Proficiency in PowerShell, Terraform, and/or Python for automation and Infrastructure-as-Code
Experience with identity and access management, including Entra ID (Azure AD), SAML, OAuth, and federated models
Familiarity with ITIL, cloud governance, and compliance frameworks for regulated industries
Exposure to Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI, Power Virtual Agents) in enterprise environments is a plus
Excellent communication and documentation skills with the ability to engage both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Demonstrated leadership and mentoring capabilities, especially in dynamic or high-pressure environments
Experience supporting pre-sales, solution scoping, and customer workshops
Willingness to travel as needed (approximately 25%–50% of the time, depending on proximity to clients)
Must be legally authorized to work in the United States for any employer without sponsorship

Preferred Certifications:

Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert
VMware Certified Professional (VCP)

Nice to Have:

Microsoft Certified: Identity and Access Administrator
Microsoft Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate
AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Professional
Google Professional Cloud Architect
Fortinet NSE or Cisco CCNP
TOGAF or ITIL Foundation
HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate
Dell EMC, NetApp, or IBM certifications related to enterprise storage and data management

Additional Information


What you should know about Resultant:

Rezzers are humble, hungry, and smart. We solve big problems, serve lots of clients, and are entirely committed to delivering transformative outcomes.
Rezzers are team players, deeply dedicated to the mission of the organization and to helping everyone around us be successful.
Resultant compensates well, rewarding performance that delivers positive outcomes for our clients and ensuring incentives are aligned to achieve our goals.
Resultant leaders work hard, serving as a shining example of what it means to be a great Rezzer. They are servant leaders, helping their team to be successful in all possible ways.
We have a great benefits package including unlimited vacation, significant 401k contributions, and several opportunities to develop yourself.
We pride ourselves in having the best talent in the industry and hope that you’re up for the challenge!

What our team members say about us…

“I love our true empathy and concern for our clients, it's very rare and appreciated. It is a pleasure to be a part of an organization like Resultant.”
“I learn something new every single day, and I feel like I'm a part of building an organization that has legs. I appreciate that I'm consistently humbled by the talent and caliber of our team.”
“The culture of the company is amazing, and the climate of my team is great. The benefits that employees are offered are better than competitors, and the one-on-one presence that my team lead gives is extremely beneficial to me.”

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, color, sex, disability, national origin, race, religion, or veteran status.


Equal Opportunity Employer

About UES

Utility Energy Services (UES) provides energy efficiency program implementation services to utilities across the U.S. We support the design, execution, and optimization of utility energy efficiency programs by leveraging data-driven insights and industry best practices.

Position Overview

UES is seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst to support performance tracking, reporting, and data analysis for utility energy efficiency programs. This role serves as a critical link between program operations, data systems, and client communication. It is ideal for an individual who is highly analytical, detail-oriented, and capable of turning complex data into clear, actionable insights.

Working closely with Program Manager, the analyst will develop and maintain Power BI dashboards, support program forecasting efforts, and contribute to client-facing deliverables. The ideal candidate will be comfortable working with structured data systems, asking thoughtful questions, and ensuring that reporting tools align with enterprise-level architecture designed by the Database Engineer on the Technology Solutions team.

This role is well suited to someone who enjoys working with data to solve problems and is curious about using AI-enabled tools and automation to enhance efficiency and insight. A willingness to learn, communicate clearly, and contribute to a collaborative, mission-driven environment is essential.

Key Responsibilities

Build and maintain Power BI dashboards, reports, and tools to support program-level performance tracking, KPI monitoring, and internal reporting
Work closely with the Program Manager to define data needs, reporting cadence, and deliverables that support forecasting, goal tracking, and program optimization
Conduct trend analysis, identify gaps or risks in program performance, and present findings that inform strategic decision-making
Collaborate with the enterprise-level Database Engineer to ensure reporting tools are based on validated, secure, and standardized data sources
Participate in the preparation and delivery of client-facing reports and presentations, including in-person meetings at client sites as needed
Leverage automation and explore the use of AI-enhanced tools to improve data workflows and insight delivery
Maintain documentation for all reporting tools, dashboards, and data processes to support consistency, scalability, and compliance
Stay informed about program requirements, regulatory changes, and performance standards to ensure reporting aligns with client and contract expectations

Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, Environmental Studies, or a related field
3+ years of experience in data analysis and reporting, preferably client-facing
Proficiency in Power BI and SQL, with demonstrated experience in building and managing dashboards and reports
Familiarity with relational databases and experience working within structured data environments
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present technical insights in a clear, client-friendly format
Interest in exploring AI-enabled tools and automation to support reporting and analytics, even without prior development experience
Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a dynamic, team-oriented environment
Willingness to travel occasionally for client presentations, workshops, or site visits
