column10:
About NexHealth

NexHealth's mission is to accelerate innovation in healthcare. Fixing healthcare is the challenge of our generation. This industry is stuck in the 1990s while affordability and accessibility are spiraling out of control. We currently spend ~20% of GDP on healthcare, while the 25% of the world's data is stuck on legacy and antiquated health systems. NexHealth's goal is to create an open and modern ecosystem to accelerate innovation in the space. We make it easier for everybody to build in healthcare, and ultimately empower ordinary people with access to more modern, efficient and affordable healthcare solutions.

Founded: 2017
Headquarters: San Francisco, CA
Funding: $177M through Series C
Employees: 190
Website: https://www.nexhealth.com/

Platform Engineering at NexHealth (Remote)

NexHealth has built the world's most advanced healthcare integration API that synchronizes in real-time. With our real-time integration with electronic health record systems (EHRs), we help developers at tech companies like Swell, Truelark, Quip, Cherry, and more quickly build products and services that synchronize with EHR systems. NexHealth has also built our own doctor facing SaaS product on top of our own API.

On the platform engineering team, you'll work on some of our most challenging problems, from building an open data lake for health records, to building dev ops for our next 100x growth, to defining and building the next generation of health record system integrations for the industry.

What You'll Do
Keep up with our exponentially growing customer base and associated dataset, scaling our data processing and storage layer from terabytes to petabytes and beyond.
Build and maintain bidirectional, real-time integrations with dozens of EHRs, scaling to hundreds or thousands of integrations.
Drive improvements to our core systems to unlock 100x growth in the number of practices, patients, and developers relying on our platform.
Own cross-functional projects end to end, from technical design and decomposition to a successful launch and rollout.
Partner with engineering leaders and product managers to generate, prioritize, and deliver on ambitious engineering goals.
What You'll Bring
10+ years of experience as a software engineer, with a focus on building scalable back-end systems and platforms.
Deep experience integrating and scaling storage systems, cloud-native services, and related open source projects, including the Kubernetes ecosystem, AWS, and PostgreSQL.
Knowledge of common DevOps tools and practices.
Firm grasp of quality and security engineering principles. You understand not just the what, but also the why.
A vision for what great software and great engineering looks like, and the technical expertise and leadership skills to make that vision a reality.
Excellent systems-level thinking. You can understand all the different components of a distributed system, how they interact, and how customer journeys and data flows touch each part.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. You can communicate technical concepts to business stakeholders, and business needs to the engineering team.
Engineering Principles

As Nexers, we are strong owners and first principle thinkers who can execute and deliver fast, hungry for learning and personal growth, and we make sure the company succeeds above all else.

As such, our culture is shaped with the following principles:

Strong ownership: Knows what needs to be done, and gets it done with little or no oversight.
Bias for action: Outcome oriented. Delivers results fast.
Acts with first principles: Describes the ideal end state with first principles, and helps us get there fast.
Growth mindset. Hungry for learning and personal growth.
Company first: Does whatever is right for the company and our customers.
Benefits
Competitive salary plus equity
Commuter benefits
401K
Full Medical, Dental and Vision
Unlimited PTO

Actual salaries will vary depending on factors including but not limited to location, experience, and performance. Other benefits may include stock options, an unlimited paid time off policy, and up to 100% coverage on medical, vision and dental insurance.

COMPANY DESCRIPTION

Insight Economics is a leading economic consulting firm that provides expert analysis in legal and business settings. Our consulting team works on complex and interesting projects with top-tier law firms and industry-leading clients, from growing businesses to Fortune 500 companies to leading universities across a variety of industries. Our work includes analysis of economic valuation and damages in matters involving intellectual property, damages, competition, antitrust, and much more. Our clients demand consistently excellent, in-depth and timely analysis, expressed in a clear and persuasive way. They depend on us to distill complex issues and communicate our results effectively in courtrooms and boardrooms across the country. Insight’s work is deeply rooted in a commitment to expertise and excellence by all team members at every level. Our team is our greatest resource, and we benefit collectively through skill development, growth opportunities, and mentorship.

Job Description – Analyst (SAN DIEGO, CA and DURHAM, NC)

Insight Economics offers excellent career opportunities to talented individuals looking to join a highly capable consulting team. We promote professional growth for each team member, recognizing each individual’s unique abilities, circumstances, and career-path preferences.  Insight provides the opportunity to build professional skills in data analysis, analytical reasoning, economic research, critical thinking, and effective writing – all through exposure to interesting projects for clients in a variety of industries. We encourage close and collegial collaboration with our experts and senior staff to enhance shared learning and development.

A significant percentage of our work involves litigation consulting, which entails providing support to attorneys and their clients involved in legal disputes, primarily through expert opinions offered in detailed written reports and testimony at trial. The expert report aims to assist attorneys, judges, and juries in understanding complex economic issues related to the case. We rigorously apply our economic expertise in areas such as case analysis, strategy development, and presentation of economic theory and complex data analysis. Our matters typically involve high-stakes disputes relating to economic harm, competitive conditions, efficiency and public interest, and valuation, among other issues. Our consultants provide critical support to Insight experts by reviewing relevant case evidence (business documents and witness testimony), analyzing complex data sets, performing statistical and quantitative analysis, and developing written reports and supporting exhibits. Consultants also assist with preparation for questioning under oath at depositions and trials, among other litigation-related projects.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Perform economic and financial research on companies, industries, products, and markets;
Evaluate industry reports, published research, company financials (e.g., balance sheet, income statement, and cash flows), and integrate that information into work product;
Help develop economic and financial models including litigation damages models, detailed discounted cash flow, net present value, product sales analysis, and other relevant analyses;
Communicate findings to other team members both orally and in writing;
Collaborate with other team members for integration of work into client deliverables;
Draft written content translating analysis results, market dynamics, and economic concepts into work product;
Create and modify content in Microsoft Office and Adobe software products;
Contribute to the development of presentations, demonstratives, expert economic and financial reports, and other client deliverables.

Analysts should have outstanding analytical and critical thinking skills, as well as a strong desire to grow professionally within the economic consulting field. We seek individuals who are comfortable with and committed to working efficiently with team members from across locations—and therefore possess strong oral and written communications skills and ability to use technology to promote an efficient working environment. Please note that this position will be based in-office in San Diego, California or Durham, North Carolina. Applicants should indicate in their cover letter their reasons for being interested in either location.

Other requirements include:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree from a leading university, preferably in Economics, Accounting, Business, or related field.
Targeting at least 3.7 GPA on a 4.0 scale.
Exceptional analytical abilities.
Excellent oral and written communication skills.
Careful attention to detail.
Experience performing economic/financial analysis, calculations, and research.
Experience performing statistical data analysis.
Driven to succeed and grow professionally.
Intellectual curiosity and desire to understand the “why” behind analysis.
Strong identification with core tenets of Insight’s work.
Please specify your experience with and/or interest in living in San Diego, CA or Durham, NC.

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $75,000.00 - $85,000.00 per year

Benefits:

401(k)
401(k) 3% Match
Dental insurance
Disability insurance
Health insurance
Life insurance
Paid time off
Vision insurance

Work Location: Hybrid remote in La Jolla, CA 92037
